description: This line chart statistically presents the average slip band (SB) spacing in ferrite (α_SB, black line), average slip band spacing plus dislocation cell block (DCB) size in austenite (γ_SB+DCB, red line), and average dislocation cell (DC) size in austenite (γ_DC, blue line) of Fe-35Mn-8Al-0.1C (8Al) low-density steel as a function of engineering strain (x-axis, range 0.00 to 0.30), with y-axis representing the size/spacing in nanometers (0 to 1400 nm). All three measured parameters show a monotonic decreasing trend with increasing engineering strain: α_SB reduces from ~760 nm at ~0.03 strain to ~80 nm at 0.28 strain, γ_SB+DCB decreases from ~900 nm at ~0.03 strain to ~220 nm at 0.28 strain, and γ_DC drops from ~260 nm at 0.20 strain to ~200 nm at 0.28 strain, with error bars indicating statistical variation of the measurements. The data from this figure are used for flow stress calculation of the 8Al steel, supporting the conclusion that continuous refinement of deformation microstructures (slip bands, dislocation cell blocks, dislocation cells) during tensile deformation contributes to the excellent strain hardening capability of the duplex low-density steel.
